user_query:
I need you to extend a small CLI to-do manager. Read everything under input/ first:
- input/README.md — project overview and conventions
- input/app.py — the current CLI implementation (supports list and filter)
- input/tasks.json — sample data (array of tasks)
- input/CONTRIBUTING.md — coding standards and non-destructive change policy

Add the following features and deliverables without asking me questions unless you hit a truly destructive change:
1) New CLI commands (additive only):
   - complete <id>: mark the task with the given numeric id as done and persist it
   - summary: print a status summary with counts of total, done, and pending, and also counts by priority
   Keep existing commands list and filter working.
2) Persistence:
   - Implement a save function to write the updated tasks back to JSON with safe/atomic behavior (write to a tmp file then replace) and basic error handling if the id isn’t found.
3) Data model and backward compatibility:
   - Ensure a boolean done field exists on tasks on load (default to false if missing) and write it on save.
4) Tests:
   - Provide unit tests that cover marking complete and computing the summary.
5) Non-destructive default:
   - Do not delete or rename existing functions; extend additively. Match naming/style conventions from the codebase. No TODO or FIXME comments anywhere.
6) Completion discipline:
   - Break the work down into a full task tree (READ/ADD/EDIT/VERIFY). Verify your own changes by re-reading files before considering them a foundation. Include a brief completion report.

Write all outputs to the output/ folder with these exact paths:
- output/src/app.py — updated CLI implementation with: load_tasks (still present), save_tasks (new), mark_complete (new), status_summary (new), and main CLI wiring for list, filter, complete, summary.
- output/data/tasks.json — updated sample data reflecting at least one completed task (done: true) and at least one pending (done: false).
- output/REPORT.md — your change report including: the full task tree with node types and statuses, a completion report enumerating verified changes, and your code read-back checklist results. Explicitly state that there are zero TODO/FIXME items.
- output/TASK_TREE.json — a machine-readable task tree: an array of nodes with fields id, title, type (READ|ADD|EDIT|VERIFY), and status. Keep this additive (no DELETE nodes).
- output/tests/test_app.py — tests for mark_complete and status_summary that use a temporary in-memory or temp-file task list so they don’t modify output/data/tasks.json during tests.

Notes:
- Use only relative paths. Do not introduce external dependencies. Keep actions additive. Match the project’s conventions and error handling style. Ensure the CLI prints helpful messages for success/failure. No TODO or FIXME strings in any output file.
